verbGlossary.ESTABLISHED DURING: {source} created and approved during the estimating and budgeting {target}
verbGlossary.SCOPED WITHIN: Project budget total value defined within the preconstruction planning effort based on program requirements
verbGlossary.RECONCILED AT: Project budget compared to actual spend and closed out during contract closeout
verbGlossary.DERIVED FROM: {source}s generated from design development documents such as drawings and specifications
verbGlossary.ALLOCATED TO: {source} dollars assigned to a specific {target} for cost tracking
verbGlossary.DISTRIBUTED ACROSS: {source} value spread across {target}'s multiple work activities to reflect phased spending
verbGlossary.STRUCTURED DURING: {source} hierarchy defined and organized during the estimating {target} to match the project breakdown
verbGlossary.TAGGED TO: {source} assigned to {target} to enable cost tracking at the activity level
verbGlossary.ORGANIZED DURING: {source} hierarchy established during preconstruction planning to align schedule and cost structures
verbGlossary.DECOMPOSED INTO: {source} broken into sub-elements corresponding to a specific {target} work packages
verbGlossary.DEFINED DURING: {source}s created during programming and feasibility to categorize anticipated project costs
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} linked to {target} to classify that phase's expenditures in the cost ledger
verbGlossary.AWARDED DURING: Subcontract commitment executed during the bidding and buyout {target} after competitive evaluation
verbGlossary.MOBILIZED FOR: {source}activated to bring the subcontractor on-site for the site setup {target}
verbGlossary.PLACED FOR: {source} issued to secure materials or equipment needed for fabrication and long-lead procurement
verbGlossary.FULFILLED DURING: {source}delivered and received on-site during the material ordering and delivery {target}
verbGlossary.PROCURED FOR: {source} created to obtain materials required for a building enclosure {target}
verbGlossary.PRICED FOR: {source} unit price and quantity set for work to be performed in a specific MEP {target}
verbGlossary.BILLED DURING: {source} invoiced for completed work during a building enclosure {target}
verbGlossary.ORIGINATED IN: {source} root cause traces back to the design development {target} where the scope was defined
verbGlossary.DISCOVERED DURING: {source} identified when unforeseen conditions emerge during {target}
verbGlossary.TRIGGERED BY: {source} caused by regulatory changes or code modifications arising from the permitting {target}
verbGlossary.COSTED AGAINST: {source} priced against the affected structural {target} budget and resources
verbGlossary.SCOPED TO: {source} scope limited to work within a specific interior rough-in {target}
verbGlossary.PROPOSED DURING: {source}submitted for pricing while a structural {target} is actively under way
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIED DURING: Potential change order recognized from conflicts or gaps discovered during the submittals review {target}
verbGlossary.APPROVED DURING: {source} approved and executed while an interior rough-in {target} is in progress
verbGlossary.ISSUED FOR: {source} formally issued to cover additional scope within a sitework {target}
verbGlossary.CHARGED AGAINST: {source} cost posted to the budget of a specific MEP {target} where the extra work occurs
verbGlossary.APPLIED TO: {source} cost attributed to a structural {target} for misc metals or rework
verbGlossary.COMPENSATED FOR: Owner change order reimburses the contractor for owner-directed changes during a site utilities {target}
verbGlossary.NEGOTIATED DURING: Owner change order terms negotiated while a building enclosure {target} reveals scope gaps
verbGlossary.REALLOCATED TO: {source} moves dollars from one cost code into {target} that needs additional funding
verbGlossary.TRANSFERRED DURING: {source} transfers contingency or allowance funds during a finishes {target} to cover overruns
verbGlossary.INCURRED DURING: {source} recorded for self-performed work or expenses during the demolition and site clearing {target}
verbGlossary.EXPENSED FOR: {source} charged as a project expense for activities in the safety and environmental orientation {target}
verbGlossary.RECORDED DURING: {source} transaction captured in the system during a finishes and trim {target}
verbGlossary.ITEMIZED FOR: {source} broken into individual charges for materials and labor in a finishes {target}
verbGlossary.POSTED DURING: {source} entered into the cost ledger while a rough-in {target} is actively progressing
verbGlossary.WITHHELD UNTIL: Owner invoice payment held back pending satisfactory completion of punch list work
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED AFTER: {source}submitted after the performance verification {target} confirms systems operate to spec
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED FOR: {source}verified and approved for payment based on work completed in a trim {target}
verbGlossary.MEASURED DURING: {source}verified against installed work during an interior rough-in {target}
verbGlossary.RELEASED AFTER: Retainage funds freed once the final inspections {target} confirms all work meets contract requirements
verbGlossary.SETTLED AT: Retainage balance fully paid out as part of the contract closeout process
verbGlossary.DISBURSED FOR: {source} issued to a subcontractor or vendor for work completed in a plumbing fixtures {target}
verbGlossary.HELD THROUGH: {source} retainage withheld throughout the warranty {target} until all warranty obligations are met
verbGlossary.COLLECTED DURING: Lien waivers gathered from all subcontractors and suppliers during contract closeout
verbGlossary.CLEARED BEFORE: {source}verified as complete before the facility is formally transitioned to the owner
verbGlossary.BROKEN DOWN DURING: {source}s created during bidding to align contract value with billable work {target}s
verbGlossary.VALUED FOR: {source} assigned a dollar value for work within an HVAC trim and controls {target}
verbGlossary.DRAWN AGAINST: Allowance dollars consumed as actual scope and pricing are determined during a finishes {target}
verbGlossary.RESERVED DURING: {source} funds set aside during preconstruction planning for unknown risks across future {target}s
verbGlossary.TAPPED DURING: {source} drawn down to cover unforeseen conditions encountered during an earthwork {target}
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED FROM: {source} baseline established from the original estimate created during the estimating {target}
verbGlossary.PROJECTED THROUGH: {source} extended to predict remaining spend through the completion of a commissioning or trim {target}
verbGlossary.FRONT-LOADED FOR: {source}projection weighted toward early spend for long-lead fabrication deposits and mobilization costs
verbGlossary.EXTENDED THROUGH: {source}projection timeline stretched to include expenditures during seasonal commissioning activities
verbGlossary.FLAGGED DURING: {source} variance identified and highlighted when a structural {target} actuals exceed the plan
verbGlossary.MONITORED THROUGH: {source} tracked through the systems startup {target} to identify late-stage overruns
verbGlossary.EVALUATED AT: {source} calculated at the integrated systems testing {target} to assess project health
verbGlossary.LOCKED DURING: {source}cost finalized when subcontracts and purchase orders are executed during bidding and buyout
verbGlossary.NORMALIZED ACROSS: {source} rate calculated per {target} to enable comparison across projects at the same stage
verbGlossary.DEPLETED BY: {source} burn rate accelerated by late-{target} changes in devices and low voltage trim installation
verbGlossary.DELAYED UNTIL: {source} processing time extended because approvals are held until owner training is complete
verbGlossary.TRUED UP AT: Cash flow variance corrected to actuals when as-built documentation confirms final installed quantities
verbGlossary.REVISED DURING: Estimate at completion updated during pre-functional testing as final system costs become clear
verbGlossary.EXPENDED ON: Allowance dollars spent on actual ceiling material and installation once selections are finalized during the finishes {target}
